description: Its brain can outperform a supercomputer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5000.

abilities.name: Damage Swap
abilities.effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to another as long as you don't Knock Out that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Alakazam is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Alakazam

description: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high-speed tackles.

abilities.name: Rain Dance
abilities.effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 1 Water Energy Card to 1 of your Water Pokémon. (This doesn't use up your 1 Energy card attachment for the turn.) This power can't be used if Blastoise is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each attached Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

name: Blastoise

description: A rare and elusive Pokémon that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to catch it.

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Chansey during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.effect: Chansey does 80 damage to itself.

name: Chansey

description: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

abilities.name: Energy Burn
abilities.effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all Energy attached to Charizard into Fire Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can't be used if Charizard is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

name: Charizard

description: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding energy cards. (No matter what type the defender is, Clefairy's type is still Colorless.)

name: Clefairy

description: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Gyarados

description: While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.
name: Hitmonchan

description: Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

abilities.name: Strikes Back
abilities.effect: Whenever your opponent's attack damages Machamp (even if Machamp is Knoced Out), this power does 10 damage to attacking Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power can't be used if Machamp is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when your opponent attacks.

name: Machamp

description: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. It frequently appears when sunspots flare up.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 80 damage to itself.

name: Magneton

description: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Mewtwo in order to use this attack. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo.

name: Mewtwo

description: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

name: Nidoking

description: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

name: Ninetales

description: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwrath but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 and discard it.

name: Poliwrath

description: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Raichu.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Raichu does 30 damage to itself.

name: Raichu

description: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to a different one. This power can't be used if Venusaur is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

name: Venusaur

description: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

name: Zapdos

description: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Beedrill

description: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

name: Dragonair

description: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dugtrio

description: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderpunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

name: Electabuzz

description: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

abilities.name: Buzzap
abilities.effect: At any time during your turn (before your attack) you may Knock Out Electrode and attach it to 1 of your other Pokémon. If you do, chose a type of Energy. Electrode is now an Energy card (instead of a Pokémon) that provides 2 energy of that type. This power can't be used if Electrode is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrode does 10 damage to itself.

name: Electrode

description: Very protective of its sprawling territory, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.effect: If Pidgeotto was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Pidgeotto to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Pidgeotto

description: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.

name: Arcanine

description: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Charmeleon in order to use this attack.

name: Charmeleon

description: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Dewgong

description: Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.
name: Dratini

description: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. This sprig is used much like a metal sword.

attacks.name: Leek Slap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Farfetch'd stays in play (even putting Farfetch'd on the Bench won't let you use it again.)

name: Farfetch'd

description: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.
name: Growlithe

description: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dream Eater
attacks.effect: You can't this attack unless the Defending Pokémon is Asleep.

name: Haunter

description: When the bulb on its back grows large, the Pokémon seems to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Ivysaur

description: Merely by meditating, the Pokémon launches a powerful psychic energy attack.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Jynx

description: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Kadabra in order use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Kadabra.

name: Kadabra

description: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Kakuna

description: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.effect: Does 50 damage minus 10 for each damage counter on Machoke.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.effect: Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

name: Machoke

description: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times number of damage counters on Magikarp.

name: Magikarp

description: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Magmar in order to use this attack.

name: Magmar

description: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Nidorino

description: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of defenders attacks. Defender cannot use that attack next turn.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times number of heads.

name: Poliwhirl

description: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

attacks.name: Conversion 1
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

attacks.name: Conversion 2
attacks.effect: Change Porygon's Resistance to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

name: Porygon

description: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.effect: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

name: Raticate

description: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.
name: Seel

description: Often hides in water to stalk unweary prey. When swimming quickly, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Wartortle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Wartortle

description: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Abra

description: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

name: Bulbasaur

description: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Caterpie

description: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Charmander in order to use this attack.

name: Charmander

description: Lives about three feet underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.
name: Diglett

description: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Doduo

description: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Drowzee

description: Almost invisible, this gaseous Pokémon cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.

attacks.name: Sleeping Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Destiny Bond
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Gastly in order to use this attack. If a Pokémon Knocks Out Gastly during your opponent's next turn, Knock Out that Pokémon.

name: Gastly

description: Because it stores several kinds of toxic gases in its body, it is prone to exploding without warning.

attacks.name: Foul Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, it is now Confused.

name: Koffing

description: Loves to build its muscles. It trains in all styles of martial arts to become even stronger.
name: Machop

description: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.

name: Magnemite

description: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Metapod during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Metapod

description: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Nidoran♂

description: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Onix (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Onix

description: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

name: Pidgey

description: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

name: Pikachu

description: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming over trying to stand.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 damage for each Energy attached to Poliwag but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Energy after the end don't count.

name: Poliwag

description: Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything flat in moments.
name: Ponyta

description: Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.
name: Rattata

description: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Sandshrew

description: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. It powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Squirtle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Squirtle

description: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value this core as a gem.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Energy card to Starmie in order to use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Starmie.

attacks.name: Star Freeze
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Starmie

description: An enigmatic Pokémon that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.
name: Staryu

description: Its whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. These vines shake as it walks.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Tangela

description: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Monster Ball, it has zapped many people.
name: Voltorb

description: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Vulpix

description: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Weedle

effect: Play Clefairy Doll as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Clefairy Doll counts as a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Clefairy Doll has no attacks, can't retreat, and can't be Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. If Clefairy Doll is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Clefairy Doll.
name: Clefairy Doll

effect: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
name: Computer Search

effect: Choose 1 of your own Pokémon in play and a Stage of Evolution. Discard all Evolution cards of that Stage or higher attached to that Pokémon. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).
name: Devolution Spray

effect: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.
name: Impostor Professor Oak

effect: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
name: Item Finder

effect: You and your opponent show each other your hands, then shuffle all the Trainer cards from your hands into your decks.
name: Lass

effect: Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can only play this card when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.
name: Pokémon Breeder

effect: Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.
name: Pokémon Trader

effect: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)
name: Scoop Up

effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon in order to choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and up to 2 Energy cards attached to it. Discard those Energy cards.
name: Super Energy Removal

effect: Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard Defender. Damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
name: Defender

effect: Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.
name: Energy Retrieval

effect: Your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.
name: Full Heal

effect: Shuffle 2 of the other cards from your hand into your deck in order to draw a card.
name: Maintenance

effect: Attach PlusPower to your Active Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard PlusPower. If this Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.
name: PlusPower

effect: Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.
name: Pokémon Center

effect: Choose 1 Basic Pokémon card from your opponent's discard pile and put it onto his or her Bench. (You can't play Pokémon Flute if your opponent's Bench is full.)
name: Pokémon Flute

effect: Look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.
name: Pokédex

effect: Discard your hand, then draw 7 cards.
name: Professor Oak

effect: Put 1 Basic Pokémon card from your discard pile onto your Bench. Put damage counters on that Pokémon equal to half its HP (rounded down to the nearest 10). (You can't play Revive if your Bench is full.)
name: Revive

effect: Discard 1 Energy card attached to your own Pokémon in order to remove up to 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.
name: Super Potion

effect: Draw 2 cards.
name: Bill

effect: Choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and discard it.
name: Energy Removal

effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
name: Gust of Wind

effect: Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
name: Potion

effect: Switch 1 of your own Benched Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
name: Switch

effect:
Provides {C}{C} energy.
Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.
name: Double Colorless Energy
